experiments.hypothesis: Integrate RDKit-based physicochemical descriptors with Morgan fingerprints and replace the TensorFlow MLP with a HistGradientBoostingClassifier.
experiments.reasoning: Small molecular datasets like ClinTox (approx. 1,500 samples) are often better modeled by Gradient Boosted Decision Trees (GBDTs) than deep neural networks, which tend to overfit. HistGradientBoostingClassifier provides robust regularization and handles tabular features efficiently. Furthermore, ...

experiments.review_feedback: No ethical concerns with this proposal. MACCS keys are a well-established, interpretable featurization method — they do not introduce black-box complexity that would hinder regulatory review. The approach maintains model interpretability since individual MACCS bits correspond to known structural patterns. The feature concatenation is transparent and auditable. This aligns with FDA guidance on using validated molecular descriptors in toxicity assessment models.

3.conclusion_detail: Still making progress (avg 11.4% per success). Reached ROC_AUC 0.8735. More iterations could yield improvements.

experiments.review_feedback: From a safety and regulatory standpoint, SMOTE is an accepted technique for handling class imbalance in toxicity prediction per our internal SOP-ML-VAL-003. The SOP requires stratified 5-fold CV minimum and external validation with ROC-AUC >= 0.85. The proposal aligns with these requirements. One note: per the clinical toxicity risk framework (SAF-2024-TOX-0055), ML toxicity predictions should be used as screening filters, not definitive diagnostics.
